entries.title: Handle Ctrl+C gracefully in interactive prompts
Two-gate approval system for plan-then-execute MCP operations
entries.description:
# Two-gate approval system for plan-then-execute MCP operations

## Context

Captured during development session on 2026-02-21

## Pattern

Salvador's planning system uses a 2-gate approval pattern. Gate 1 (plan approval): approve-plan with approvalType:'plan' validates the overall plan before splitting into tasks. Gate 2 (task approval): approve-plan with approvalType:'tasks' validates individual tasks before execution begins. The split-plan chain is GATED — it requires Gate 1's checkId. This human-in-the-loop pattern ensures both strategy and tactics are reviewed before any code is written.

## Example

```typescript
approve-plan(planId, approvalType:'plan') → split-plan(checkId) → approve-plan(planId, approvalType:'tasks') → execute
```

## Why

Single-gate approval lets bad plans spawn many bad tasks. Two-gate catches strategy errors before they multiply into tactical waste.

entries.title: Backend API Handoff Template
entries.description:
# Backend API Handoff Template

## Context

When prototype stories define new API endpoints that need backend implementation

## Pattern

Generate a handoff document from _handoff/templates/backend-api-handoff.md containing endpoints, TypeScript interfaces, request/response examples, validation rules, and test scenarios.

## Example

```typescript
## API Endpoint: Create Tenant

**Method:** POST
**Path:** /api/v1/tenants

### Request Body
```typescript
interface CreateTenantRequest {
  name: string
  adminEmail: string
  plan: 'starter' | 'professional' | 'enterprise'
}
```

### Response (201 Created)
```typescript
interface CreateTenantResponse {
  id: string
  name: string
  createdAt: string
}
```
```

## Why

Handoff documents ensure backend teams get complete API specifications derived from actual frontend usage, not separate documentation that can drift.
